user_request: Design a realistic two-stage Miller-compensated opamp in 50nm CMOSedu using gm/Id + LUT-aware sizing and LTspice verification.
design_story: Product-level analog demo that builds on the matured common-source amplifier and differential-pair flows. This V1 is a realistic short-channel 50nm low-power two-stage opamp demo; the prior 30MHz UGB goal is deferred to a high-speed V2 because the valid LUT-backed candidate space reached about 15.26MHz while satisfying gain and output-bias requirements.
presentation_expectations: Show spec targets versus measured values.
Show AC gain and phase with UGB and phase-margin markers.
Show unity-gain step response with settling band and slew-rate annotation.
Show device gm/Id operating-point summary with region/saturation margins.
Show complete sizing/bias conditions for schematic reproduction.
